nodes.name: fm.scenario.state.pollution
nodes.summary:
If a scenario's cleanup steps fail (service left stopped, iptables rule not
cleared, etcd key not deleted), the next scenario starts from a broken baseline.
The next scenario may fail for a completely unrelated reason, producing a
misleading failure report. The root failure is in the cleanup, not the next test.

nodes.name: cluster.ca.generated.once
nodes.summary:
scripts/entrypoint.sh generates the cluster CA only when running as node-1
(GLOBULAR_CA_MODE=generate). All other nodes copy the CA from the shared volume
(GLOBULAR_CA_MODE=copy). The CA must never be regenerated after the cluster is
bootstrapped — all existing service certs are signed by the original CA and will
fail mTLS verification if the CA changes. Regenerating the CA is a full
cluster teardown, not a fix.

nodes.name: awareness.proposals.draft.only
nodes.summary:
Quickstart has documented lab exceptions (external ScyllaDB, relaxed MinIO quorum,
single-node etcd for some smoke tests) that do not apply to production clusters.
A failure learning proposal generated from a quickstart training scenario may
encode lab-exception behavior as a production invariant. All quickstart-originated
proposals must remain in DRAFT state until a human reviewer has verified that the
encoded rule is safe for production.

nodes.name: HARD RULES — NEVER VIOLATE
nodes.summary: HARD RULES — NEVER VIOLATE

nodes.name: BUILD COMMANDS
nodes.summary: BUILD COMMANDS

nodes.name: KNOWN ISSUES (check before assuming things work)
nodes.summary: KNOWN ISSUES (check before assuming things work)

nodes.name: cluster.etcd.quorum.awareness
nodes.summary:
Scenarios in tests/scenarios/catastrophic/ deliberately reduce etcd quorum to
test cluster behavior under failure. These scenarios must assert the expected
outcome (etcd enters read-only mode, controller stops writing desired state)
rather than asserting cluster health. A scenario that kills 2 of 3 etcd nodes
and then asserts cluster.healthy = true is a broken test, not a passing one.

nodes.name: metric_warning:workflow_failed_runs_15m
nodes.summary: Workflow failures indicate convergence risk and may threaten reconcile fairness

metadata.explanation: Workflow failures indicate convergence risk and may threaten reconcile fairness

nodes.name: metric_warning:minio_offline_disks
nodes.summary: Offline MinIO disks risk artifact availability and violate topology contract

metadata.explanation: Offline MinIO disks risk artifact availability and violate topology contract

nodes.name: testharness.scenario_isolation
nodes.summary:
Scenarios must not assume the cluster is in a known-good state from a prior
scenario. Every scenario must verify its baseline preconditions before executing
and must restore the cluster to a clean state in its cleanup steps.
Tests that pollute state for subsequent scenarios are false negatives: the next
test appears to fail for the wrong reason, masking real bugs.

nodes.name: fm.layer.collapse.in.test.assertion
nodes.summary:
A test that checks /globular/nodes/{id}/packages/SERVICE/node-agent and sees an
entry concludes "node agent is running". But Layer 3 only records what was
installed, not what is currently running. A crash-recovery test that uses this
assertion will show pass even when the service is dead. The test is measuring
the wrong layer.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: This file is read automatically by Claude Code at the start of every session. It contains the rules, invariants, and operational knowledge needed to work safely with the Globular codebase.

nodes.name: AI MEMORY SERVICE
nodes.summary: AI MEMORY SERVICE

nodes.name: fm.hosts.not.seeded.before.services
nodes.summary:
If entrypoint.sh fails partway through, exits early, or is bypassed, systemd
may start services before /etc/hosts contains the cluster peer entries. Services
that resolve peer hostnames (e.g. node-2, node-3) at startup fail with
"name or service not known" because the DNS service itself is not yet running
and /etc/hosts is the only resolution path at this point.

nodes.name: cluster.dns.seeded.before.services
nodes.summary:
Services perform peer discovery using hostnames (node-1, node-2, etc.) before
the DNS service is running. If /etc/hosts is not pre-seeded by entrypoint.sh,
peer hostname resolution fails and services cannot connect, causing a cascade
of startup failures. entrypoint.sh must complete its /etc/hosts seeding before
systemd starts any service unit.

nodes.name: metric_warning:scylla_disk_percent
nodes.summary: ScyllaDB disk pressure risks workflow/AI memory backend availability

metadata.explanation: ScyllaDB disk pressure risks workflow/AI memory backend availability

nodes.name: fm.probe.mutation.breaks.idempotency
nodes.summary:
If a probe function in probes.sh calls etcdctl put, docker exec to modify files,
or systemctl to start/stop services, then calling the probe a second time changes
the cluster state. Subsequent probes return different results depending on how
many times the probe was called. The test harness calls probes at baseline,
after each step, and in assertions — a single mutating probe corrupts all of
these readings.

nodes.name: fm.sigterm.crash.test.false.pass
nodes.summary:
systemctl stop sends SIGTERM, which causes a graceful shutdown with exit code 0.
systemd's Restart=on-failure only triggers on crash signals or non-zero exit.
A crash recovery test that uses systemctl stop will: (1) stop the service cleanly,
(2) systemd will NOT restart it, (3) the test then checks if the service came back
up, which it did not. The test fails — but for the wrong reason: the test itself
is broken, not the service.

nodes.name: testharness.probes_readonly
nodes.summary:
The 1,049-LOC probes.sh library is the read-only observation layer of the test
harness. Every function in it must be safe to call repeatedly without changing
cluster state. No etcdctl put, no docker exec that modifies files, no systemctl
commands that start/stop services may appear inside a probe function.
Chaos and mutations are separate YAML action steps, never inside probes.
Violating this makes tests non-idempotent: a second probe call changes the
result of a subsequent probe call.

nodes.name: metric_warning:etcd_disk_percent
nodes.summary: etcd disk full triggers NOSPACE alarm, blocks all writes, halts convergence

metadata.explanation: etcd disk full triggers NOSPACE alarm, blocks all writes, halts convergence

nodes.name: testharness.sigkill.for.crash.tests
nodes.summary:
systemctl stop sends SIGTERM, which causes a graceful exit (exit code 0).
systemd's Restart=on-failure does NOT trigger on a clean exit — only on
non-zero exit or signal death. A crash recovery test that uses systemctl stop
or SIGTERM will never exercise the auto-restart path. Use SIGKILL (kill -9 or
pkill -SIGKILL) to simulate a real crash and trigger Restart=on-failure.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: CLAUDE.md

nodes.name: metric_warning:etcd_fsync_latency_ms
nodes.summary: High etcd fsync latency risks leader election instability and workflow dispatch timeouts

metadata.explanation: High etcd fsync latency risks leader election instability and workflow dispatch timeouts

nodes.name: fm.ca.regenerated.midcluster
nodes.summary:
If scripts/entrypoint.sh regenerates the CA key (openssl genpkey + openssl req)
on a container that was already running, the new CA public key does not match
the certificate authority that signed the existing service certs. Every mTLS
handshake in the cluster fails: the peer presents a cert signed by the old CA,
but the verifier only trusts the new CA. The cluster becomes completely
disconnected.

nodes.name: cluster.layer3.not.layer4
nodes.summary:
An etcd record in /globular/nodes/{node_id}/packages/ proves that the package
was installed on that node — not that the service is currently running. The test
harness must always check both Layer 3 (etcd installed record) and Layer 4
(systemctl status) independently. Assertions that check only one layer produce
false positives: a service can be installed but crashed, disabled, or missing its
unit file.

nodes.name: fm.lab.exception.promoted.to.production
nodes.summary:
Quickstart has three documented lab exceptions: external ScyllaDB, relaxed MinIO
quorum (1-2 nodes for smoke), and single-node etcd in some training scenarios.
If an awareness training scenario generates a proposal from behavior observed
under a lab exception, and that proposal is auto-promoted, the production
awareness graph may encode "MinIO can run on 1 node" or "ScyllaDB is external"
as a valid production state. This would suppress correct warnings about
production quorum violations.

nodes.name: COMMON MISTAKES TO AVOID
nodes.summary: COMMON MISTAKES TO AVOID

invariants.title: Cluster CA must be generated exactly once (on node-1) and never regenerated
invariants.summary:
scripts/entrypoint.sh generates the cluster CA only when running as node-1
(GLOBULAR_CA_MODE=generate). All other nodes copy the CA from the shared volume
(GLOBULAR_CA_MODE=copy). The CA must never be regenerated after the cluster is
bootstrapped — all existing service certs are signed by the original CA and will
fail mTLS verification if the CA changes. Regenerating the CA is a full
cluster teardown, not a fix.

invariants.title: /etc/hosts must be seeded with peer IPs before any service starts
invariants.summary:
Services perform peer discovery using hostnames (node-1, node-2, etc.) before
the DNS service is running. If /etc/hosts is not pre-seeded by entrypoint.sh,
peer hostname resolution fails and services cannot connect, causing a cascade
of startup failures. entrypoint.sh must complete its /etc/hosts seeding before
systemd starts any service unit.

invariants.title: Installed state (etcd Layer 3) must never be treated as proof of running (Layer 4)
invariants.summary:
An etcd record in /globular/nodes/{node_id}/packages/ proves that the package
was installed on that node — not that the service is currently running. The test
harness must always check both Layer 3 (etcd installed record) and Layer 4
(systemctl status) independently. Assertions that check only one layer produce
false positives: a service can be installed but crashed, disabled, or missing its
unit file.

invariants.title: Crash recovery tests must use SIGKILL, not SIGTERM or systemctl stop
invariants.summary:
systemctl stop sends SIGTERM, which causes a graceful exit (exit code 0).
systemd's Restart=on-failure does NOT trigger on a clean exit — only on
non-zero exit or signal death. A crash recovery test that uses systemctl stop
or SIGTERM will never exercise the auto-restart path. Use SIGKILL (kill -9 or
pkill -SIGKILL) to simulate a real crash and trigger Restart=on-failure.

invariants.title: Awareness proposals generated from quickstart training are DRAFT; require manual review before production promotion
invariants.summary:
Quickstart has documented lab exceptions (external ScyllaDB, relaxed MinIO quorum,
single-node etcd for some smoke tests) that do not apply to production clusters.
A failure learning proposal generated from a quickstart training scenario may
encode lab-exception behavior as a production invariant. All quickstart-originated
proposals must remain in DRAFT state until a human reviewer has verified that the
encoded rule is safe for production.

invariants.title: Test scenarios that reduce etcd quorum must document expected read-only mode
invariants.summary:
Scenarios in tests/scenarios/catastrophic/ deliberately reduce etcd quorum to
test cluster behavior under failure. These scenarios must assert the expected
outcome (etcd enters read-only mode, controller stops writing desired state)
rather than asserting cluster health. A scenario that kills 2 of 3 etcd nodes
and then asserts cluster.healthy = true is a broken test, not a passing one.

invariants.title: All probe functions must be read-only — no mutations inside a probe
invariants.summary:
The 1,049-LOC probes.sh library is the read-only observation layer of the test
harness. Every function in it must be safe to call repeatedly without changing
cluster state. No etcdctl put, no docker exec that modifies files, no systemctl
commands that start/stop services may appear inside a probe function.
Chaos and mutations are separate YAML action steps, never inside probes.
Violating this makes tests non-idempotent: a second probe call changes the
result of a subsequent probe call.

invariants.title: Each scenario run must start from a verified baseline and restore it on exit
invariants.summary:
Scenarios must not assume the cluster is in a known-good state from a prior
scenario. Every scenario must verify its baseline preconditions before executing
and must restore the cluster to a clean state in its cleanup steps.
Tests that pollute state for subsequent scenarios are false negatives: the next
test appears to fail for the wrong reason, masking real bugs.

failure_modes.title: Crash test uses systemctl stop — Restart=on-failure never triggers — test always passes
failure_modes.summary:
systemctl stop sends SIGTERM, which causes a graceful shutdown with exit code 0.
systemd's Restart=on-failure only triggers on crash signals or non-zero exit.
A crash recovery test that uses systemctl stop will: (1) stop the service cleanly,
(2) systemd will NOT restart it, (3) the test then checks if the service came back
up, which it did not. The test fails — but for the wrong reason: the test itself
is broken, not the service.
symptoms: Crash recovery scenario always fails at the "service restarted" assertion
journalctl shows clean exit (code=exited, status=0) not a crash
Removing Restart=on-failure from unit file has no effect on test outcome
failure_modes.root_cause:
systemctl stop sends SIGTERM, which causes a graceful shutdown with exit code 0.
systemd's Restart=on-failure only triggers on crash signals or non-zero exit.
A crash recovery test that uses systemctl stop will: (1) stop the service cleanly,
(2) systemd will NOT restart it, (3) the test then checks if the service came back
up, which it did not. The test fails — but for the wrong reason: the test itself
is broken, not the service.

failure_modes.title: Quickstart lab exception encoded as production invariant via awareness proposal
failure_modes.summary:
Quickstart has three documented lab exceptions: external ScyllaDB, relaxed MinIO
quorum (1-2 nodes for smoke), and single-node etcd in some training scenarios.
If an awareness training scenario generates a proposal from behavior observed
under a lab exception, and that proposal is auto-promoted, the production
awareness graph may encode "MinIO can run on 1 node" or "ScyllaDB is external"
as a valid production state. This would suppress correct warnings about
production quorum violations.
symptoms: Production awareness graph shows invariant MinIO quorum >= 1 node
Awareness stops warning about missing storage profiles on founding nodes
A quickstart-origin proposal appears in production graph without DRAFT marker
failure_modes.root_cause:
Quickstart has three documented lab exceptions: external ScyllaDB, relaxed MinIO
quorum (1-2 nodes for smoke), and single-node etcd in some training scenarios.
If an awareness training scenario generates a proposal from behavior observed
under a lab exception, and that proposal is auto-promoted, the production
awareness graph may encode "MinIO can run on 1 node" or "ScyllaDB is external"
as a valid production state. This would suppress correct warnings about
production quorum violations.

failure_modes.title: Probe function contains a mutation — test results change on re-run
failure_modes.summary:
If a probe function in probes.sh calls etcdctl put, docker exec to modify files,
or systemctl to start/stop services, then calling the probe a second time changes
the cluster state. Subsequent probes return different results depending on how
many times the probe was called. The test harness calls probes at baseline,
after each step, and in assertions — a single mutating probe corrupts all of
these readings.
symptoms: Test passes on first run, fails on second run of the same scenario
Probe function shows in etcd diff output between baseline and assertion
A chaos scenario passes even when the chaos step was skipped
failure_modes.root_cause:
If a probe function in probes.sh calls etcdctl put, docker exec to modify files,
or systemctl to start/stop services, then calling the probe a second time changes
the cluster state. Subsequent probes return different results depending on how
many times the probe was called. The test harness calls probes at baseline,
after each step, and in assertions — a single mutating probe corrupts all of
these readings.

failure_modes.title: Scenario cleanup fails — next scenario inherits partial or broken cluster state
failure_modes.summary:
If a scenario's cleanup steps fail (service left stopped, iptables rule not
cleared, etcd key not deleted), the next scenario starts from a broken baseline.
The next scenario may fail for a completely unrelated reason, producing a
misleading failure report. The root failure is in the cleanup, not the next test.
symptoms: Scenario B fails but the logs show a problem that should have been from Scenario A
cluster.health probe returns false at the start of a new scenario
docker exec to a node shows unexpected iptables rules or stopped services
failure_modes.root_cause:
If a scenario's cleanup steps fail (service left stopped, iptables rule not
cleared, etcd key not deleted), the next scenario starts from a broken baseline.
The next scenario may fail for a completely unrelated reason, producing a
misleading failure report. The root failure is in the cleanup, not the next test.

failure_modes.title: CA key regenerated after bootstrap — all service certs become invalid
failure_modes.summary:
If scripts/entrypoint.sh regenerates the CA key (openssl genpkey + openssl req)
on a container that was already running, the new CA public key does not match
the certificate authority that signed the existing service certs. Every mTLS
handshake in the cluster fails: the peer presents a cert signed by the old CA,
but the verifier only trusts the new CA. The cluster becomes completely
disconnected.
symptoms: All inter-service gRPC calls fail with TLS handshake error or certificate not trusted
etcd peer connections fail (etcd cannot achieve quorum)
Restarting any service does not help (its cert was signed by the old CA)
failure_modes.root_cause:
If scripts/entrypoint.sh regenerates the CA key (openssl genpkey + openssl req)
on a container that was already running, the new CA public key does not match
the certificate authority that signed the existing service certs. Every mTLS
handshake in the cluster fails: the peer presents a cert signed by the old CA,
but the verifier only trusts the new CA. The cluster becomes completely
disconnected.

failure_modes.title: Services start before /etc/hosts is populated — peer hostname resolution fails
failure_modes.summary:
If entrypoint.sh fails partway through, exits early, or is bypassed, systemd
may start services before /etc/hosts contains the cluster peer entries. Services
that resolve peer hostnames (e.g. node-2, node-3) at startup fail with
"name or service not known" because the DNS service itself is not yet running
and /etc/hosts is the only resolution path at this point.
symptoms: Node agent fails to start with connection refused or name resolution error
etcd cannot form quorum because peer URLs (node-2:2380) are unresolvable
scripts/seed-etcd.sh fails because cluster-controller is unreachable
failure_modes.root_cause:
If entrypoint.sh fails partway through, exits early, or is bypassed, systemd
may start services before /etc/hosts contains the cluster peer entries. Services
that resolve peer hostnames (e.g. node-2, node-3) at startup fail with
"name or service not known" because the DNS service itself is not yet running
and /etc/hosts is the only resolution path at this point.

failure_modes.title: Test asserts Layer 3 (etcd installed) and concludes Layer 4 (service running) — false positive
failure_modes.summary:
A test that checks /globular/nodes/{id}/packages/SERVICE/node-agent and sees an
entry concludes "node agent is running". But Layer 3 only records what was
installed, not what is currently running. A crash-recovery test that uses this
assertion will show pass even when the service is dead. The test is measuring
the wrong layer.
symptoms: Resilience scenario passes but manual check shows service is not running
probe_service_status returns ok but systemctl status shows inactive
Crash injection step appears to have no effect (test still passes)
failure_modes.root_cause:
A test that checks /globular/nodes/{id}/packages/SERVICE/node-agent and sees an
entry concludes "node agent is running". But Layer 3 only records what was
installed, not what is currently running. A crash-recovery test that uses this
assertion will show pass even when the service is dead. The test is measuring
the wrong layer.
